summary: Recent updates to Firefox's integration with **WebRTC**, the technology behind video calls, focus on enhancing the way the browser handles video codecs and media capabilities. The integration of the H.264 FMTP (Format Parameter Set) parser improves how the browser communicates its capabilities for video streaming, ensuring better compatibility with various video formats. Additionally, improvements to the accuracy of media capability support queries—based on resolution and framerate—will enhance the browser's ability to adapt to different streaming conditions, potentially providing users with smoother video experiences. Configuring audio send stream bitrate and bandwidth allocation also aims to optimize audio quality during calls. Together, these changes make Firefox better equipped for efficient video communication in real-time situations, which is increasingly important as online interactions continue to grow.
